purpose: Machine-readable grouping of the Phase 3.25 GrammarProblems mining ledger into the nahw issues that must be taught, gated, and cited before Phase 4 corpus-facing grammar authoring.

clusters.label: Issue #1 - right answer with wrong i'rab/reasoning is unsafe
clusters.production_rule: A grammar-sensitive hover or entry repair is unsafe unless the conclusion and the syntactic reason both pass.
clusters.sarf_lesson: Do not let a correct-looking lexical match bypass form, voice, mood, or norm_strict checks.
clusters.nahw_lesson: Reject conclusion-only i'rab, case, mood, exception, or deep essay reasoning even when the final English answer looks plausible.
clusters.learner_explanation: The reason is part of the answer. If the parse is wrong, the gloss cannot be trusted.
clusters.future_action: Keep all deep, essay, analysis, evaluation, generation, and wrong-reasoning-trap cases behind answer-plus-reasoning review.

clusters.label: Issue #2 - function, attachment, and role ambiguity
clusters.production_rule: Function words and phrase roles must preserve their syntactic contribution before a hover can propagate.
clusters.sarf_lesson: Segment proclitics and hosts, but defer the meaning of particles, prepositions, and attachment to nahw.
clusters.nahw_lesson: Classify idafa, jar-majrur, hal, tamyiz, badal, istithna, tanazu, and PP attachment before authoring the visible hover.
clusters.learner_explanation: A small particle or attachment can change what the whole token teaches.
clusters.future_action: Route function and attachment cases to exact token/parse evidence; keep ambiguous attachment pending instead of using a host-only gloss.

clusters.label: Issue #3 - morphosyntax and visible-token preservation
clusters.production_rule: The visible hover must preserve morphology that materially changes the token: voice, form, mood, case, suffix pronoun, and clitic contribution.
clusters.sarf_lesson: Preserve form, voice, derived type, suffix pronouns, and strict vocalized surface before proposing a gloss.
clusters.nahw_lesson: Use case, mood, governing particle, and role to decide whether the morphology can be rendered safely or must stay pending.
clusters.learner_explanation: The same root can teach different things when form, voice, case, or suffix changes.
clusters.future_action: Do not collapse a composed or inflected token into a lemma gloss; surface each material grammatical contribution or leave a precise pending.

clusters.label: Correct positive regression - direct cases remain safe only under existing gates
clusters.production_rule: Direct cases may remain positive controls, but auto_safe still requires strict surface, source-address, and no grammar-context dependency.
clusters.sarf_lesson: A positive control proves the guard can accept simple rows; it does not authorize norm-only propagation.
clusters.nahw_lesson: Even direct grammar rows are only safe when the construction has no unresolved context dependency.
clusters.learner_explanation: Some cases are straightforward, but they still pass through the same evidence ladder.
clusters.future_action: Keep these as controls; do not promote them into broad closure evidence.

clusters.label: New root-cause cluster requiring a new issue
clusters.production_rule: Any mined failure not covered by Issues #1-#3 must create a named issue before Phase 4 authoring proceeds.
clusters.sarf_lesson: Name the morphology failure before adding fixes.
clusters.nahw_lesson: Name the syntax failure before adding fixes.
clusters.learner_explanation: A new failure pattern becomes a lesson, drill, regression, and validator before it becomes production policy.
clusters.future_action: Move the ledger row to new_root_cause_cluster with proposed_issue_id, then add procedure, drill, fixture, and validator before corpus authoring.
